name: TET2 CHIP canakinumab vascular-inflammation registry
summary: Recruiting randomized double-blind placebo-controlled phase 2 study of canakinumab in coronary artery disease with and without TET2 clonal hematopoiesis, measuring vascular inflammation and TET2 variant allele fraction.
population: Adults and older adults with coronary artery disease, including a planned half of participants with TET2 clonal hematopoiesis

trial_details.expected_results_window: The local record lists 2030-04-01 as the study end date; do not assume results until a registry or publication update is reviewed.
trial_details.horizon_note: No posted results are represented locally, so surveillance should watch for registry results or linked publications.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers and safety in Adults and older adults with coronary artery disease, including a planned half of participants with TET2 clonal hematopoiesis. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
